conjecture_title: Informal: characterization of graphs with the strong transversal property
summary: The question of which graphs H have the strong transversal property — i.e., for which H there exists epsilon > 0 guaranteeing a B-transversal copy of H in every graph with an (epsilon W, epsilon W)-cohesive blockade of local degree less than epsilon W — is posed as an open problem in Section 2 of the source paper. The paper establishes that H must be a forest, proves the property for paths and certain caterpillar-like trees, and shows it fails for trees containing a vertex of degree d with 2^{d-1} >= |H|. No complete characterization has been found, and no follow-up paper resolving or substantially extending this question was found in five targeted web searches covering arXiv and the published literature through May 2026.
search_queries: Scott Seymour Spirkl "strong transversal property" "pure pairs" transversal trees forest
arxiv 2111.00532 "transversal trees" "cohesive blockade" strong transversal property characterization
WebFetch https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.05713 (On density conditions for transversal trees in multipartite graphs)
WebFetch https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06354 (Transversals in a collections of trees)
notes: Two superficially related arXiv papers were checked: arXiv:2305.05713 studies density thresholds for connected/Hamiltonian transversals in multipartite graphs (a different setting, not blockades/local-degree conditions), and arXiv:2310.06354 studies rainbow transversals in families of trees — neither addresses the strong transversal property as defined in 2111.00532. The source paper was published in SIAM J. Discrete Math. 38(1), 2024, pp. 645–667. No follow-up found.
